metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a named test (e.g. `test_digest_not_in_corpus`) to `tests/unit/test_plan_review_overlap.py` that asserts overlap findings are returned when the query ticket is absent from the retrieval corpus, and cite it in AC2.
findings.finding: AC2 ('The query digest is generated at review time from the ticket's current full text, independent of the ticket being in the enriched corpus') has no named test case or proving command; an executor cannot demonstrate this criterion is satisfied.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC2
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Testing section: `test_digest_not_in_corpus` — fixture corpus contains peer tickets but NOT the query ticket; assert `overlap[]` is non-empty and digest was derived from the review-time full text. Reference this test in AC2.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Remove or correct the 'its output schema' clause for the MCP `review_plan` result: the tool is documented NO_SCHEMA_EXEMPT (plain dict, no outputSchema) — `overlap[]` will appear in the returned dict automatically; the schema deliverable is only the JSON Schema file (`plan_review_verdict.schema.json`), not an MCP outputSchema.
findings.finding: The plan says to include `overlap[]` in 'the MCP `review_plan` result + its output schema', but the MCP `review_plan` tool is explicitly NO_SCHEMA_EXEMPT (returns a plain dict with no outputSchema by design, per _mcp_llm.py:174 and schemas/__init__.py:110). There is no outputSchema to update for this tool.
findings.location: What section / Scope bullet: 'include overlap[] in the MCP review_plan result + its output schema'
findings.suggested_fix: Change 'include overlap[] in the MCP review_plan result + its output schema' to 'overlap[] appears in the MCP review_plan result dict (NO_SCHEMA_EXEMPT; no separate MCP outputSchema); the JSON Schema deliverable is plan_review_verdict.schema.json'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Name at least one test function (e.g. `test_cli_text_render` and `test_mcp_schema_includes_overlap`) in the Testing section and cite them in AC4 so the rendering and schema coverage are concretely provable.
findings.finding: AC4's CLI-rendering and MCP-schema sub-criteria ('`overlap[]` is rendered in CLI `review-plan --output text` and present in the MCP `review_plan` result + output schema') cite no named test function, leaving verification implicit.
findings.location: Acceptance Criteria — AC4; Testing section
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Testing section: `test_cli_text_render` — call `_render_plan_review_text` with a verdict containing a populated `overlap[]` and assert the substring 'rebar link' appears in stdout; `test_mcp_schema_includes_overlap` — assert `overlap` key is present in the MCP output-schema dict. Cite both in AC4.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add a named test case (e.g., `test_empty_candidates`) in `tests/unit/test_plan_review_overlap.py` that asserts: when retrieval returns zero candidates (no exception), `overlap[]` is an empty list and the verdict is unchanged.
findings.finding: The 'empty candidates' branch (retrieval succeeds but returns zero results) is mentioned in plan prose as a graceful-skip trigger but has no dedicated named test case — it is a distinct code path from the exception path covered by `test_runtime_error`.
findings.location: Testing section — named tests list; also 'empty candidates' mention in 'LLM-gate / graceful skip' prose
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Testing section: `test_empty_candidates` — FakeRunner returns an empty list from retrieve (no exception); assert `result['overlap'] == []` and verdict byte-identical to overlap-disabled run.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Define the normative field vocabulary for each overlap[] item in the plan (at minimum: candidate_ticket_id, overlap_summary, rebar_link_command, and any score/confidence field from S6) and add a corresponding `overlap` array property with an `items` sub-schema to `plan_review_verdict.schema.json`.
findings.finding: The overlap[] item schema (each element's required fields) is never defined — the plan says each item = 'the S6 finding incl. its rebar link command' but neither the plan nor the target schema file defines what fields an overlap item carries (e.g., candidate_ticket_id, similarity_score, overlap_summary prose, rebar_link_command string). The existing plan_review_verdict.schema.json uses additionalProperties:true throughout but doesn't document the overlap[] items' vocabulary at all.
findings.location: What section / Scope section — 'add overlap[] to src/rebar/schemas/plan_review_verdict.schema.json'; AC line 1: 'returns ≤3 overlap findings in a dedicated overlap[] key (each with a rebar link command)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to plan: 'Each overlap[] item shape: {candidate_ticket_id: str, overlap_summary: str, rebar_link_command: str, score: float (0–1 from S6 judge)}'. Add to schema: `"overlap": {"type": "array", "description": "...", "items": {"type": "object", "properties": {"candidate_ticket_id": {"type": "string"}, "overlap_summary": {"type": "string"}, "rebar_link_command": {"type": "string"}, "score": {"type": "number"}}, "additionalProperties": true}}`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the config flag name: align `verify.overlap_enabled` (body/Scope) with `overlap.enabled` (AC4) by picking one canonical dotted name and using it consistently in all sections.
findings.finding: Terminology drift between body and AC: the config flag is called `verify.overlap_enabled` in the What/Config/Scope sections but `overlap.enabled` in AC4. An implementer reading AC4 as the ground truth would name the field differently from what the body specifies, causing a test/config mismatch.
findings.location: What section (Config paragraph) vs Acceptance Criteria AC4
findings.suggested_fix: Use `overlap_enabled` as the Python attribute name on `VerifyConfig` (consistent with the `[tool.rebar][verify]` TOML section), and update AC4 to read '`verify.overlap_enabled` (default OFF)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Canonicalize the overlap gate flag name: pick ONE name used consistently in What, Config, AC, and code (e.g. `overlap_enabled` as a `VerifyConfig` field, accessed as `[tool.rebar.verify] overlap_enabled = true`) and update all three plan sections that currently use different forms ('verify.overlap_enabled', 'overlap.enabled').
findings.finding: The plan asserts `verify.overlap_enabled` will be added to `VerifyConfig` in `[tool.rebar][verify]`, but the config key is named `overlap_enabled` in the Config section and `verify.overlap_enabled` in the What section — an inconsistency — and the field does not yet exist (confirmed absent from VerifyConfig at _config_schema.py:209-295). The plan also names it 'overlap.enabled' in the AC. Three different names are used for the same flag.
findings.location: Config section ('verify.overlap_enabled'), What section ('overlap.enabled' in AC line 4), and VerifyConfig
findings.suggested_fix: Use `overlap_enabled: bool = False` as the VerifyConfig field name (matching the snake_case convention of remediation_mode, completion_floor_active, etc.), referenced in toml as `[tool.rebar.verify] overlap_enabled = true`, and update the AC to match.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Reconcile the config key name in both the Config paragraph and AC line 4 to a single consistent form (e.g. `verify.overlap_enabled`) matching the `VerifyConfig`/`_SECTIONS['verify']` pattern used by all peer flags.
findings.finding: The plan names the config flag inconsistently: it is called `verify.overlap_enabled` in the Config paragraph but `overlap.enabled` in AC line 4, making the exact TOML key ambiguous for both implementers and testers.
findings.location: Config paragraph vs. Acceptance Criteria line 4
findings.suggested_fix: Change AC line 4 to read '`verify.overlap_enabled` (default OFF) gates the step independently' and ensure the Config paragraph uses the same dotted key.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Harmonize the config flag name across all plan sections: use `verify.overlap_enabled` (a flat bool on `VerifyConfig`, consistent with all analogous flags) everywhere, and update AC4 to match.
findings.finding: The config flag name is inconsistent between the plan body (calls it `verify.overlap_enabled` on `VerifyConfig` at `[tool.rebar][verify]`) and AC4 (calls it `overlap.enabled`). These imply different config topologies: `verify.overlap_enabled` maps to a flat boolean on `VerifyConfig` (matching the pattern of every analogous gate flag: `remediation_mode`, `completion_floor_active`, `progressive_drift_refresh`, `enable_code_review`), while `overlap.enabled` implies a new top-level `[overlap]` TOML section and a new `OverlapConfig` dataclass—a structurally different and non-standard approach given the codebase pattern.
findings.location: AC4: '`overlap.enabled` (default OFF) gates the step independently' vs Config section: '`verify.overlap_enabled` (default OFF, on `VerifyConfig` `[tool.rebar][verify]`'
findings.suggested_fix: In AC4, replace '`overlap.enabled` (default OFF)' with '`verify.overlap_enabled` (default OFF, a flat bool on `VerifyConfig` at `[tool.rebar][verify]`, consistent with `remediation_mode`, `completion_floor_active`, etc.)'.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Clarify the injection timing: `coverage['counts']` is baked inside `finalize_verdict` (orchestrator.py:500), which is called within `produce_plan_review_verdict` before `_run_plan_review` gets control — the overlap injection happens *after* counts are fixed; update the plan prose to say 'inject after `produce_plan_review_verdict` returns but before sidecar emit' (the separate `overlap[]` key already reflects the correct design).
findings.finding: The plan asserts overlap findings should be injected 'BEFORE coverage[counts] is baked' in _run_plan_review, but coverage['counts'] is already baked inside finalize_verdict (orchestrator.py:500), which is called within produce_plan_review_verdict before _run_plan_review receives the verdict dict. The injection point in _run_plan_review is AFTER counts are fixed.
findings.location: What section / integration site: '_run_plan_review (:401)'; also 'BEFORE coverage[counts] is baked'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace 'inject overlap findings BEFORE coverage[counts] is baked' with 'inject overlap findings into the verdict dict returned by produce_plan_review_verdict, after counts are fixed but before the sidecar emit and attestation signing' to match the actual code structure.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Correct the sequencing claim in the 'What' section: replace 'BEFORE `coverage[\'counts\']` is baked' with 'AFTER `produce_plan_review_verdict` returns (counts are already frozen inside the workflow; the separate `overlap[]` key is intentionally excluded from coverage counts)' to match the actual injection site.
findings.finding: The plan's preamble states overlap findings should be injected 'BEFORE `coverage[\'counts\']` is baked'—but in the actual codebase, `coverage['counts']` is assembled and frozen INSIDE `produce_plan_review_verdict()` (orchestrator.py:500), which returns a fully-formed verdict before `_run_plan_review` can inject anything. The injection point (:401) is post-verdict, so the 'before counts is baked' sequencing claim is factually incorrect and could lead an implementer to attempt a pre-verdict injection (e.g., by modifying `produce_plan_review_verdict` or the workflow ops), fighting the architecture unnecessarily.
findings.location: ## What — first bullet: 'inject overlap findings BEFORE `coverage[\'counts\']` is baked'
findings.suggested_fix:
Replace: 'inject overlap findings BEFORE `coverage["counts"]` is baked and BEFORE the sidecar emit + attestation signing'
With: 'inject overlap findings AFTER `produce_plan_review_verdict` returns and BEFORE the sidecar emit + attestation signing (coverage["counts"] is already frozen inside the workflow; the separate `overlap[]` key is intentionally excluded from it)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify precisely what text S1 uses to generate the query ticket's digest: `PlanContext.plan_text` (title + description only) vs a broader concatenation including children — and reconcile with the 'full-text context' phrase, since `plan_text` excludes child ticket content.
findings.finding: The plan says 'extract the query ticket's digest from the already-assembled full-text context (S1)', implying the full-text context includes all ticket content. However, `PlanContext.plan_text` (the assembled context exposed to plan-review steps) is only `f"{self.title}\n\n{self.description}"` — it excludes children content. If S1 digest generation operates on `plan_text`, it silently omits child ticket content that is part of the plan.
findings.location: What section: 'synchronously extract the query ticket's digest from the already-assembled full-text context (S1)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the plan which `rebar link` relation type(s) the S6 judge should assign to overlap findings (e.g., always `relates_to`, or judge-selected from {relates_to, duplicates}), or explicitly note that S6's spec owns this rule and add a forward reference.
findings.finding: The 'rebar link command' embedded in each overlap item requires the S6 judge to select a relation type from the fixed vocabulary (blocks | depends_on | relates_to | duplicates | supersedes | discovered_from), but the plan never specifies which relation the judge should recommend or how it maps overlap findings to that vocabulary — leaving an undefined enum-selection rule inside S6's output that this story consumes.
findings.location: What section — 'each item = the S6 finding incl. its rebar link command'; AC line 1: 'each with a rebar link command'
findings.suggested_fix: Add: 'The rebar link command uses relation=relates_to by default (S6 may escalate to duplicates when similarity score > 0.9); the integration story constructs the command as rebar link <query_ticket_id> <candidate_ticket_id> <relation>.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Resolve the sequencing contradiction: either (a) state that overlap[] injection happens AFTER counts are baked (since it doesn't affect them or the attestation), giving the canonical placement relative to the existing steps in _run_plan_review, or (b) clarify what 'before counts' achieves if overlap[] is excluded from counts.
findings.finding: The sequencing rule 'inject overlap findings BEFORE coverage[counts] is baked' conflicts with the chosen approach 'a separate overlap[] key excluded from ... the signed coverage counts'. If overlap[] is fully separate and excluded from counts, its injection order relative to 'counts is baked' is irrelevant — but the plan presents both as simultaneously true without resolving the contradiction, leaving the implementer with an ambiguous placement rule.
findings.location: What section — 'Sequencing is critical: inject overlap findings BEFORE coverage[counts] is baked and BEFORE the sidecar emit + attestation signing' vs 'chosen approach: a separate overlap[] key so ... the attestation is unaffected'
findings.suggested_fix: Replace the conflicting phrase with: 'Overlap findings are appended to verdict["overlap"] AFTER gate_dispatch.produce_plan_review_verdict() returns and BEFORE sidecar.emit() — their separate key means counts and attestation are unaffected regardless of insertion order.'

findings.finding: Acceptance-criteria / definition-of-done quality issues.
findings.suggested_fix: Split compound criteria, replace subjective terms with observable outcomes, and state how each is verified.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify how timeouts are *enforced* on S5/S6 calls (e.g., a named constant `OVERLAP_STEP_TIMEOUT_S`, passed to `asyncio.wait_for` or the HTTP client), not just that `TimeoutError` is caught.
findings.finding: No explicit timeout *enforcement* mechanism is specified for the S5 retrieve and S6 judge calls. The plan lists 'retrieval timeout' as a caught exception but does not specify how or where the timeout deadline is imposed (e.g., asyncio.wait_for, httpx timeout param, a deadline constant). Without enforcement, a hung LLM or retrieval call will block _run_plan_review — and therefore the entire plan-review command — indefinitely.
findings.location: What section / LLM-gate / graceful skip paragraph: 'RUNTIME failures too (S1/S5/S6 exception, retrieval timeout, judge error, empty candidates) are caught'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the spec: 'S5 and S6 calls are wrapped with `asyncio.wait_for(..., timeout=OVERLAP_STEP_TIMEOUT_S)` (default 10 s, configurable via `verify.overlap_timeout_s`); TimeoutError is caught and treated as empty overlap[].'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Before implementation, document the concrete API contracts (function signatures, return types, and failure modes — raise vs. return-error-dict) for S1, S5, and S6 in this plan or in a shared interface spec, and verify the graceful-skip catch strategy matches all three failure modes.
findings.finding: The plan asserts specific integration contracts for S1 (digest extraction), S5 (retrieve), and S6 (pairwise judge) — including exception boundary semantics (raise on error) — but these are unwritten prerequisite stories with no existing code; their actual APIs are unverified at plan time.
findings.location: ## Dependencies — 'S1 (enrich), S5 (retrieve), S6 (judge). These are prerequisite sibling stories'; ## What — 'synchronously extract…run retrieve (S5), run the pairwise judge (S6)'; ## Testing — 'a judge exception → empty overlap[]'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the CLI rendering rule for empty/absent `overlap[]`: e.g., 'omit the section entirely when overlap[] is absent or empty; only render the section header when overlap[] contains ≥1 item.'
findings.finding: The CLI rendering behavior for an empty or absent `overlap[]` is unspecified. When `overlap_enabled=True` but no similar tickets are found (0 results), `_render_plan_review_text` could either omit the section entirely or print an empty header — the plan does not specify which. An empty section header would be confusing UX; silent omission is preferable but needs to be stated.
findings.location: What / Steps: '_render_plan_review_text (src/rebar/_cli/_llm_commands.py:386) to print the overlap[] section'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the spec: 'The overlap section is only rendered in CLI text output when overlap[] is non-empty; an empty or absent overlap[] key produces no output in the text renderer.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Once S1/S5/S6 land, verify and name their specific exception types in this story's implementation spec so the catch clause is correctly scoped (not just `except Exception` without the BLE001 rationale).
findings.finding: The plan catches 'S1/S5/S6 exception' generically, but since S1/S5/S6 are prerequisite stories that have not yet landed, their actual exception types are unverified. A mismatch between the catch clause and the real exception type (e.g. catching Exception broadly vs a specific LLMUnavailableError vs a retrieval-specific error) could silently swallow a programming error rather than a genuine runtime failure, or could miss a specific exception that should propagate.
findings.location: What section: 'RUNTIME failures too (S1/S5/S6 exception, retrieval timeout, judge error, empty candidates) are caught → empty overlap[], logged, never block'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Confirm whether each `overlap[]` item should carry a pre-rendered `rebar link` CLI string or a structured `{source_id, target_id, relation}` triple from which the CLI renders the command, to avoid CLI-version coupling inside stored finding objects.
findings.finding: Each `overlap[]` item is specified to include 'its `rebar link` command' as a field. This embeds a CLI invocation string inside a structured finding object — a pattern that does not exist in any current finding shape (`blocking[]`, `advisory[]`, `coaching[]`, `dropped[]`). The existing finding schema (`plan_review_verdict.schema.json`) uses structured fields (`criteria`, `finding`, `severity`, `priority`, `id`); the coaching step uses `guide_url`. Embedding a CLI command string in a finding item is a novel, unvalidated pattern that is schema-invisible (`additionalProperties: true`) and risks CLI-version coupling.
findings.location: What section: 'each item = the S6 finding incl. its `rebar link` command'; Acceptance Criteria: 'each with a `rebar link` command'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add `src/rebar/_config_schema.py` (VerifyConfig dataclass attribute + `_SECTIONS['verify']` coercer entry for `overlap_enabled`) and `docs/config.md` to the Scope edit list so the config flag is fully wired and documented.
findings.finding: `verify.overlap_enabled` (the new config flag) requires edits to `src/rebar/_config_schema.py` — both the `VerifyConfig` dataclass and the `_SECTIONS['verify']` coercer dict — but neither file appears in the plan's Scope edit list.
findings.location: Scope section; Config paragraph ('overlap_enabled on VerifyConfig [tool.rebar][verify]')
findings.suggested_fix: In the Scope bullet, append: `src/rebar/_config_schema.py` (add `overlap_enabled: bool = False` to `VerifyConfig` and `'overlap_enabled': lambda v, k: _as_bool(v, k)` to `_SECTIONS['verify']`), and `docs/config.md` (document the new flag alongside the other verify flags).

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] State the expected retrieve candidate pool size (e.g. 'call retrieve with top_k=10; judge then reduces to ≤3') so the wiring code can be written without consulting the S5 story.
findings.finding: The plan does not state how many candidates S5 (`retrieve`) returns before S6 (`judge`) reduces them to ≤3, leaving the interface contract between the integration code and the prerequisite stories ambiguous for the executor writing the wiring.
findings.location: What section — 'run retrieve (S5), run the pairwise judge (S6); attach the ≤3 results'
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the What section: 'Call `retrieve(digest, top_k=10)`; pass all returned candidates to `judge`, which emits the top ≤3 findings for `overlap[]`.' Adjust the number to match S5's defined interface.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Explicitly specify `overlap[]` as an OPTIONAL (non-required) field in the schema addition, and note this in Scope or the ADR to confirm backward-compat with existing stored verdicts.
findings.finding: The schema change to `plan_review_verdict.schema.json` does not specify whether `overlap[]` is an optional or required field. If it is added as required, all previously stored/emitted verdict documents that lack the key will fail schema validation — a breaking change with no rollback path stated.
findings.location: Scope section: '`src/rebar/schemas/plan_review_verdict.schema.json` (add `overlap[]`)'
findings.suggested_fix: Add to the Scope or AC: 'Add `overlap[]` as an optional array field (no `required` constraint) in `plan_review_verdict.schema.json` to preserve backward compatibility with stored verdicts and gracefully-skipped runs.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an `overlap_max_findings` (or equivalent) entry to `VerifyConfig` alongside `overlap_enabled` so the ≤3 cap is configurable rather than hardcoded.
findings.finding: The ≤3 overlap-findings cap is hardcoded in the plan with no config entry, while other gate thresholds (e.g., `require_plan_review_for_claim`, `overlap_enabled`) are explicitly made configurable via `[tool.rebar][verify]`. This cap is likely to evolve as the feature matures.
findings.location: What section / Scope section — '≤3 results' / 'overlap[]' key description
findings.suggested_fix: Add `overlap_max_findings: int = 3` to `VerifyConfig` under `[tool.rebar][verify]`, reference it in the `_run_plan_review` injection site, and note it in the ADR alongside `overlap_enabled`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify the log level and at least one structured field (e.g., `logger.warning('overlap step failed', exc_info=True, extra={'step': 'S5|S6', 'reason': str(e)})`) for all caught exceptions in the overlap step, and consider a metric counter for monitoring.
findings.finding: Observability for the new overlap step is under-specified: the plan says failures are 'logged' but does not name the log level, logger, or any metric/counter for monitoring. Operators have no specified way to detect that the overlap step is silently failing at scale (e.g., misconfigured API key, retrieval service down).
findings.location: What section / LLM-gate / graceful skip paragraph: 'caught → empty overlap[], logged, never block'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Rename `verify.overlap_enabled` to `verify.enable_overlap` (or confirm `overlap_enabled` intentionally departs from the `enable_<noun>` convention used by `verify.enable_code_review`) to keep the `[verify]` section naming consistent.
findings.finding: The plan introduces `verify.overlap_enabled` to `VerifyConfig`, but the established naming convention for the equivalent toggle in the same section is `enable_<noun>` (e.g., `verify.enable_code_review`). The departure creates two naming conventions in one config section, making discoverability harder for operators and creating an implicit config-surface inconsistency.
findings.location: What section / Config: `verify.overlap_enabled` (default OFF) on VerifyConfig `[tool.rebar][verify]`
findings.suggested_fix: Use `enable_overlap: bool = False` in `VerifyConfig` and `verify.enable_overlap` in TOML, matching `enable_code_review`.

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add an 'Alternatives considered' note explaining why the existing `scan_epics_for_spec` / `spec-alignment` overlap reviewer (src/rebar/llm/spec_scan.py) cannot serve as the cross-ticket overlap mechanism, and justify the new S1/S5/S6 retrieval pipeline as the preferred approach.
findings.finding: The plan introduces a new retrieve+judge overlap pipeline without considering the existing `scan_epics_for_spec` / `spec-alignment` reviewer already in the codebase that explicitly detects scope overlaps across tickets — no rationale is given for why that existing mechanism cannot be adapted.
findings.location: ## What — 'Steps: synchronously extract the query ticket's digest…run retrieve (S5), run the pairwise judge (S6)'
findings.suggested_fix: In the ## Why section, add a paragraph: 'The existing spec_scan / spec-alignment path is a BATCH operation over all open epics against an external spec text — it is not driven per-ticket and has no retrieval or digest step. The new pipeline is ticket-local and retrieval-gated, so it fires only for semantically similar candidates; the two paths are complementary. The spec_scan reviewer is not reused because [reason].'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify S5's retrieval backend and any auth preconditions it introduces (e.g. a separate embedding API key), and confirm the 'API key absent → silent no-op' skip condition covers all required credentials for S1/S5/S6.
findings.finding: S5 ('retrieve') is described as a retrieval step but the plan gives no information about what backend it uses (embedding API, vector DB, BM25, etc.). If it involves a new external API (e.g. an embedding provider endpoint), that is a new integration surface with its own auth preconditions, rate limits, and failure modes — none of which are stated in this plan.
findings.location: Dependencies section: 'S5 (retrieve)'; also What section: 'run `retrieve` (S5)'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Declare the API key injection mechanism for the S1/S5/S6 LLM calls (e.g., env-var `REBAR_LLM_API_KEY` read at call time via the existing secrets-provider pattern) and explicitly forbid storing the key in `VerifyConfig` / `pyproject.toml` / any committed file.
findings.finding: The plan specifies 'API key absent → silent no-op' as a graceful-skip condition but never declares HOW the API key is injected or stored on the boundary-crossing S1/S5/S6 path (env var, secrets manager, config file, etc.). This leaves the secret lifecycle undeclared at the one trust-boundary crossing the plan introduces.
findings.location: What section / Config bullet: 'verify.overlap_enabled (default OFF, on VerifyConfig)' and graceful-skip description ('if the [agents] extra, plan-review, or API key is absent, the step is a silent no-op')
findings.suggested_fix: Add one sentence to the Config section: 'The API key is sourced exclusively from the environment variable `REBAR_LLM_API_KEY` (or the project's existing secrets-provider); it MUST NOT appear in `VerifyConfig`, `pyproject.toml`, or any committed file. The existing secrets-access helper used by plan-review SHALL be reused.'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Specify in the Testing/Scope section that exception-path logging for S1/S5/S6 MUST redact Authorization headers and limit logged fields to error type + sanitized message only (no request body, no headers); add a unit assertion in `test_plan_review_overlap.py::test_runtime_error` that the log record contains no API key substring.
findings.finding: The plan states that S1/S5/S6 runtime failures are 'caught → empty overlap[], logged, never block', but does not specify what is included in the log payload. On the outbound LLM API call path, exception objects can capture HTTP response bodies, request headers (including Authorization), or full prompt text; logging these without redaction would expose the API key and potentially sensitive ticket content in log sinks.
findings.location: What section / graceful-skip: 'RUNTIME failures too (S1/S5/S6 exception, retrieval timeout, judge error, empty candidates) are caught → empty overlap[], logged, never block'

findings.checklist_item: - [ ] Add to the plan a one-line interface contract for S1's output as consumed by this story (e.g., 'S1 returns a dense vector embedding / text hash / structured digest object of type X'), or add a forward reference to S1's spec that defines its return type.
findings.finding: The S1 step ('extract the query ticket's digest from the already-assembled full-text context') references a 'digest' whose format and computation are never defined in this plan. S1 is a prerequisite sibling story; this integration plan treats its output as given, but the field/type/algorithm of the digest is not specified here — making the graceful-skip and exception-handling fallback for S1 formally ambiguous (the implementer cannot know what to try before catching).
findings.location: What section — 'Steps: synchronously extract the query ticket's digest from the already-assembled full-text context (S1)'

metrics.claim_path: no-llm/no-network (structural; the fast claim check is a local HMAC verify)
